title: Data Cleanup With Audit Loop
summary: Cleans production-like data only after defining allowed records, audit logs, and rollback safeguards.
steps: Write the allowed-record definition and owner approval before touching mutable data.
Run a dry-run classification and sample both records to keep and records to change.
Fix the classification logic until samples match the definition and edge cases are documented.
Execute changes in batches with an audit trail, backup, and rollback procedure.
Verify remaining data and update prevention logic so the problem does not recur.
stopConditions: cleanup complete and prevention in place
author: Palette Lab
prompt:
Use the Data Cleanup With Audit Loop for [dataset, allowed_definition, dry_run_query].

First define the task boundary, success criteria, allowed actions, required evidence, risk level, and budget. Work in bounded iterations. In each iteration:
- Write the allowed-record definition and owner approval before touching mutable data.
- Run a dry-run classification and sample both records to keep and records to change.
- Fix the classification logic until samples match the definition and edge cases are documented.
- Execute changes in batches with an audit trail, backup, and rollback procedure.
- Verify remaining data and update prevention logic so the problem does not recur.

Quality gates before completion:
- definition approved
- dry-run reviewed
- backup or rollback exists
- batch audit captured
- post-cleanup verification passes

Stop when one of these is true:
- cleanup complete and prevention in place
- sample review shows ambiguous records
- approval missing

Finish with:
- definition
- dry-run counts
- sample review
- audit log
- verification query

Escalate for human approval before: deleting data, modifying customer records, changing compliance-retained records.
